spec_match.rationale: The playground now uses the current CSS Gap Decorations property names from the spec: rule-break, rule-inset, rule-overlap, rule-visibility-items, row-rule, column-rule, and repeat() list values.

interactive.rationale: Layout tabs, range controls, selects, color controls, generated CSS, live style updates, and a CSS.supports() panel all update from the page controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index card and concept lede now name the same current spec controls demonstrated by the page.

edge_cases.rationale: The page exposes unsupported-state detection with CSS.supports(), zero-width rule settings, negative and positive insets, none/normal/intersection break modes, overlap ordering, and visibility modes.

references.rationale: The page links to chromestatus.com/feature/5157805733183488 and the CSS Gap Decorations spec.

design_system.rationale: The added UI uses existing CSS variables, keeps button background and text colors paired, and avoids new raw CSS color literals.

summary: The major reference blocker and spec-surface issues are addressed. The script now parses, the demo uses current CSS Gap Decorations terminology, and the UI includes runtime support and boundary controls. Chrome DevTools MCP was not available in this session, so no browser-verification claim is made here.
